rulings.Case Title: STEPHAN HOWSEPIAN ET AL VS. SALMAN SHARIAT
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 19, 2025 line 1. PETITIONER STEPHAN HOWSEPIAN, JULIA CHOPE MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD is continued to March 6, 2025 for Petitioner to provide a properly bound and tabbed copy of the Request for Judicial Notice no later than February 21, 2025. The unbound and untabbed copy may be picked-up from the Court's outbox. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JALASAI BAPU, LLC VS. RAMON MARTINEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Directing The Clerk To Enter Defendant'S Default
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 19, 2025 line 12. PLAINTIFF JALASAI BAPU, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Directing The Clerk To Enter Defendant'S Default is DENIED. Plaintiff's complaint does not allege compliance with or exemption from SFRO. Plaintiff is not required to use the optional UD form complaint but should use it for guidance as to the required allegations. This motion is denied without prejudice for Plaintiff to seek a court default. Unlike the clerks, the Court is not limited to performing ministerial duties and may interpret allegations in the complaint. Plaintiff may file and serve an amended complaint including TPA 2019 allegations (see UD-100, sections 7 and 8 for guidance) and seek a clerk's default, if appropriate. Alternatively, Plaintiff may file a motion for entry of default by the Court advancing legal arguments as to why this particular complaint is sufficient. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
